sanskrit:
ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम्।
मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः॥
transliteration: ye yathā māṃ prapadyante tāṃs tathaiva bhajāmyaham | mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ ||
literal_meaning: In whatever way people surrender to Me, in exactly that way I reciprocate. All human beings, O Pārtha, follow My path in every respect.
contextual_meaning: The psyche mirrors back whatever attitude we bring to the Self; every relational stance—devotion, fear, curiosity, avoidance—activates a corresponding facet of the inner divine, shaping both our experience and our growth trajectory.
psychological_state: Secure-base phenomenon: the Self is experienced as a reliably responsive attachment figure whose feedback loops validate and refine the ego’s relational stance.
trigger_scenario: A client realizes their inner critic softens into a coach the moment they stop berating themselves and instead ask, 'What are you trying to protect me from?'
anchor_text: Whatever feeling you bring to the Self, the Self returns in kind.
